name: Lower Aragón and Arga Rivers Loop
name_original: Alfaro, Peralta, Marcilla, Villafranca – Ruta Circular
description: This moderate 37.3-mile touring cycling route connects Alfaro, Peralta, Marcilla, and Villafranca, offering varied landscapes and river view
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alfaro traverse a diverse landscape shaped by the Ebro and Alhama Rivers, featuring undulating valleys and rocky hills. The region is characterized by extensive vineyards and includes significant natural features like the Reserva Natural de Los Sotos de Alfaro. Cyclists can explore riverside ecosystems, groves, and meanders, with routes often connecting cultural landmarks and monumental villages. Best touring cycling routes around Alfaro The most popular touring cycling route is Alfaro, Peralta, Marcilla, Villafranca – Circular Route, a 37.3 miles (60.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours and 15 minutes to complete. This route offers a journey through varied landscapes, connecting several towns. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Embalse de la Estanca – Cueva de la Mora loop from Alfaro, a moderate 33.3 miles (53.6 km) path. This route leads through natural areas, including the Embalse de la Estanca, offering views of the local flora and fauna. Local touring cyclists also love the St. Michael's Collegiate Church – San Francisco Church loop from Alfaro, a 24.4 miles (39.3 km) trail leading through Alfaro's surroundings, often completed in about 3 hours and 10 minutes. This route provides an opportunity to see local landmarks and the Ebro river basin. Touring cycling around Alfaro is defined by the Ebro river basin, extensive vineyards, and natural reserves, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage. The network includes options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Alfaro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alfaro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alfaro?
A: Alfaro offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 90 options to explore. These routes cater to different skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Alfaro?
A: The terrain around Alfaro is diverse, shaped by the Ebro and Alhama Rivers. You'll find undulating valleys, rocky hills, and extensive vineyards. Many routes traverse riverside ecosystems, groves, and meanders, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Alfaro?
A: Yes, Alfaro has several easy touring cycling routes suitable for families. These often follow paths along the Ebro river, providing tranquil rides through natural vegetation and fruit trees. The region's natural reserves, like the Reserva Natural de Los Sotos de Alfaro, offer gentle paths perfect for a leisurely family outing.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while cycling around Alfaro?
A: While cycling, you can explore significant natural features such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5982980" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sotos del Ebro Nature Reserve in Alfaro&lt;/a&gt;, known for its riverside forests and meanders. Other notable natural monuments include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3598457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Roscas&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2791578" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalén Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, offering varied landscapes and viewpoints.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Alfaro?
A: Yes, many routes around Alfaro are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e969284981/alfaro-peralta-marcilla-villafranca-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfaro, Peralta, Marcilla, Villafranca – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, connecting several towns through varied landscapes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40014257" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de la Estanca – Cueva de la Mora loop from Alfaro&lt;/a&gt;, which leads through natural areas.

Q: What cultural attractions can I visit along the cycling routes?
A: Alfaro is rich in cultural heritage. You can visit the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5625705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;St. Michael's Collegiate Church&lt;/a&gt;, famous for hosting the world's largest urban colony of white storks. The historic town center also features churches and palaces, and you might encounter the Roman Nymphaeum. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31819466" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;St. Michael's Collegiate Church – San Francisco Church loop from Alfaro&lt;/a&gt; is a great route to see local landmarks.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Alfaro?
A: The touring cycling routes around Alfaro are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 50 reviews. Cyclists often praise the blend of natural beauty, such as the Ebro river basin and vineyards, with the cultural heritage found in the towns and villages.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. Alfaro offers 46 difficult touring cycling routes for those seeking a challenge. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10464780" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Santa María de Yerga – View of the Cliffs Near Nájera loop from Rincón de Soto&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant elevation changes and panoramic views.

Q: Can I cycle through the famous La Rioja wine region from Alfaro?
A: Yes, Alfaro is situated within the renowned La Rioja wine region. Many cycling routes traverse extensive vineyards, offering picturesque scenery and opportunities to experience the area's viticultural heritage. You'll find both paved surfaces and hard-packed gravel paths suitable for exploring the wine country.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Alfaro?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, the region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for touring cycling. These seasons offer milder temperatures, perfect for enjoying the vineyards, riverside paths, and cultural sites without the intense heat of summer or the chill of winter.

Q: Are there any routes that explore the unique badlands landscape near Alfaro?
A: For a distinct landscape, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850100" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardenas Reales Badlands – Monument to the Bardenas Shepherd loop from Castejón de Ebro&lt;/a&gt;. This challenging route takes you through the unique, semi-desert landscape of the Bardenas Reales, offering a stark contrast to the riverside and vineyard trails closer to Alfaro.
tips:
The Peñalén Ravine, also known as the King's Ravine, is a stunning natural spot located about 7 km from Funes, in the Ribera Alta region of Navarre.
The viewpoint offers a vertical drop of approximately 392 m, where on clear days you can see the Bardenas Reales, Villafranca, Marcilla, and even the Pyrenees.
A small town of about 6,000 people. In its old town there are various palaces and houses from the Baroque era, with beautiful facades.

The final stretches of the Arga and Aragón rivers have become an ecological corridor for plants, birds, fish, reptiles and mammals. Among them is the European mink, one of the most endangered mammal species on the planet.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Alfaro, Peralta, Marcilla, Villafranca – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: The route is designed for touring bicycles and generally takes around 4 hours and 15 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The Alfaro, Peralta, Marcilla, Villafranca – Circular Route is classified as moderate, making it suitable for a range of fitness levels. While some climbs might be present, much of the terrain is well-paved.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a mixed terrain, with a significant portion being well-paved or asphalt. The landscape features xerophytic views composed of gypsum and eroded clay, especially from viewpoints near Peralta.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Alfaro, Peralta, Marcilla, Villafranca – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery, including views of the Bardenas Reales, Villafranca, Marcilla, and even the Pyrenees on clear days. You'll also pass by the confluence of the Aragón River and Arga River before they flow into the Ebro. Other highlights include the Peñalén Gorge and the Old Town of Peralta.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: While specific recommendations aren't provided, the region's climate suggests spring and autumn would offer the most pleasant temperatures for cycling. Clear days will enhance views of distant landmarks like the Pyrenees.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Alfaro, Peralta, Marcilla, Villafranca – Circular Route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route connecting several towns, you can typically find parking in Alfaro, Peralta, Marcilla, or Villafranca to start and end your journey conveniently.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. You'll find sections that are part of the Camino de la Vera Cruz (El camino de los Templarios), Camino Jacobeo del Ebro, Camino natural de Ebro - Tramo central, and Ruta ciclable del Visón, among others.

faqs.question: Is the Alfaro, Peralta, Marcilla, Villafranca – Circular Route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through natural areas, specific regulations regarding dogs are not detailed. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in towns and near wildlife, and to check local signage.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the LIC Tramos Bajos del Aragón y del Arga. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling through this natural area, but always respect local regulations and conservation efforts.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, since the route connects the towns of Alfaro, Peralta, Marcilla, and Villafranca, you will find various options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation within these communities.

faqs.question: What kind of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given that a significant portion of the route features well-maintained, paved surfaces, a touring bicycle is ideal for this circular route.
